KINGSLEY IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR DISMISSAL OF THE CLASS ALLEGATIONS WITHOUT PREJUDICE AND THE INDIVIDUAL CLAIM OF KURT HAGGSTROM WITH PREJUDICE" IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 30, 2013 PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. NO USEFUL PURPOSE IS SERVED BY REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO AMEND. AS TO THE FIRST CLAIM FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, AT THE VERY LEAST, THAT CLAIM ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO NOMINAL DAMAGES. (CACI 3.60). THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CLAIM FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT IS BASED ON THE ASSUMPTION THAT THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CLAIM WILL BE SUSTAINED. AS TO THE DEFINITION OF THE CLASS, THERE IS NO DOUBT THAT PLAINTIFF INTENDED TO DEFINE THE CLASS AS EXCLUDING POLICIES COVERED BY ERISA. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. (COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER MIRRORING THE COURT'S RULING AND EMAIL TO COURT- COPYING OPPOSING COUNSEL- FOR SIGNATURE). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KURT HAGGSTROM'S MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S INDIVIDUAL CLAIM WITH PREJUDICE AND DISMISS THE CLASS ACTION CLAIM WITHOUT PREJUDICE - MR. KINGSLEY SEEKS CLARIFICATION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH REGARDS TO THE CLASS. THE COURT WILL REVIEW THE PAPERS AND ADVISE COUNSEL OF ITS RULING. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR #6185 (302/EHG) **AFTER HEARING, THE COURT REVIEWS THE MOTION AND ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND APPLICATION COMPLIES W/ CRC 3.770. THE COURT SIGNS DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER. ALL COUNSEL NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING VIA TELEPHONE. **

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KURT HAGGSTROM'S MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL THE "UNDER SEAL DECLARATION OF GEORGE R. KINGSLEY IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR DISMISSAL OF THE CLASS ALLEGATIONS WITHOUT PREJUDICE AND THE INDIVIDUAL CLAIM OF KURT HAGGSTROM WITH PREJUDICE IS GRANTED, FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. THE DECLARATION OF GEORGE KINGSLEY MAY BE FILED UNDER SEAL. A REDACTED COPY OF THE DECLARATION WILL BE FILED IN THE PUBLIC RECORD. ONLY THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT IS TO BE REDACTED IN THE DECLARATION AND THE ATTACHED EXHIBIT. THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHTS OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THIS PORTION OF THE RECORD; THE INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORD; THE PROPOSED ORDER IS NARROWLY TAILORED, AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXISTS TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. ONLY THE DOLLAR AMOUNT OF THE SETTLEMENT IS SEALED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To File Under Seal The "Under Seal Declaration Of George R. Kingsley In Support Of Pltff'S Mtn For Dismissal Of The Class Allegations Without Prejudice And The Individual Claim Of Kurt Haggstrom With Prejudice;"
Off Calendar Aug-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
George R. Kingsley, Esq. of Kingsley & Kingsley, APC, Tel. 818.990.8300 for plaintiff and moving party by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The Court adopted in its entirety the tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF KURT HAGGSTROM'S Motion To File Under Seal The "Under Seal Declaration Of George R. Kingsley In Support Of Plaintiff's Motion For Dismissal Of The Class Allegations Without Prejudice And The Individual Claim Of Kurt Haggstrom With Prejudice is granted, for good cause shown. The Declaration of George Kingsley may be filed under seal. A redacted copy of the declaration will be filed in the public record. Only the settlement amount is to be redacted in the declaration and the attached exhibit. There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the rights of public access to this portion of the record; the interest supports sealing the record; the proposed order is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exists to achieve the overriding interest. Only the dollar amount of the settlement is sealed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Nov-08-2012 Continued To Jan-16-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ERIC KINGSLEY, ESQ., KINGSLEY & KINGSLEY, 818.990.8300, FOR PLAINTIFF KURT HAGGSTROM.
AMY BRIGGS, ESQ., MANATT, PHELPS & PHILIPS, 415.291.7451, FOR DEFENDANT BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. No useful purpose is served by requiring plaintiff to amend. As to the first claim for breach of contract, liberally construed, at the very least, that claim adequately alleges that plaintiff is entitled to nominal damages. (CACI 3.60). The demurrer to the second claim for breach of the implied covenant is based on the assumption that the demurrer to the first claim will be sustained. As to the definition of the class, there is no doubt that plaintiff intended to define the class as excluding policies covered by ERISA. Defendant's request to take judicial notice is denied. (counsel for plaintiff to prepare a form of order mirroring the court's ruling and email to court- copying opposing counsel- for signature). Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
